source_old_req: Assume that the runtime computational resource allocation is in a legal and deadlock-free steady state, as indicated by the Task component's resource_allocation_legal and resource_allocation_deadlock_free output ports being true. Guarantee that during the continuous loop invocation of the robot main program block by the Task.impl_1 thread, the thread's health status remains within a safe boundary, as indicated by the Task component's thread_health_status_safe output port being true, ensuring that the robot's non-real-time continuous control logic is not unexpectedly interrupted due to resource exhaustion.
natural_requirement: The Task component shall execute according to the scheduling and timing constraints defined for Task.impl_1. The implementation shall use the Background dispatch protocol.

requirement_items.text: The Task.impl_1 implementation shall execute using the Background dispatch protocol.
requirement_items.source_text_before_behavior_style_rewrit: The Task.impl_1 implementation shall assign Dispatch_Protocol as Background.

quality_policy.traceable: Every item has trace_refs and expected_check metadata tied to AADL facts.
quality_policy.principle: Every generated sentence must name an explicit property assignment or structural connection; ordinary feature declarations are not used as rebuilt requirements.

natural_requirement_before_behavior_styl: The Task.impl_1 implementation shall assign Dispatch_Protocol as Background.

behavior_style_policy.basis: front-110 AGREE-derived requirements favor implementable behavior, constants, assumptions, guarantees, routing, and bounded properties.
behavior_style_policy.rule: Do not describe unsupported validity/legal-boundary semantics unless the AADL model exposes variables, values, or properties that can implement them.

natural_requirement_before_paragraph_rew: The Task.impl_1 implementation shall execute using the Background dispatch protocol.

paragraph_style_policy.rule: Req.txt is written as a natural-language requirement paragraph; per-item traceability stays in requirement_items.
paragraph_style_policy.basis: Front-110 AGREE-derived requirements generally combine function, assumptions, guarantees, and implementation notes into 1-4 sentence paragraphs.
